{{Quote|I just tinker with stuff I've found lying around the ruins on Yavin IV, or that the Trandoshans and smugglers bring me. Of course, with them it's usually weapons and things... But I just have so many ideas that people haven't seemed to come up with yet. It's really quite astonishing.|Suvam Tan to Revan about his work|<ref name="KotOR" />}}
'''Suvam Tan''' was a [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] merchant who operated an abandoned [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] [[Yavin Station|space station]] around [[Yavin Prime/Legends|Yavin]] around the time of the [[Jedi Civil War]]. After [[Revan/Legends|Revan's]] redemption, he and Tan became friends. Revan would visit his station to trade and play [[Pazaak/Legends|pazaak]], and during his quest for the Star Forge, helped him drive off a few [[Trandoshan/Legends|Trandoshan]] thugs. At first, they left, but later, they equipped themselves with [[Stealth field generator/Legends|Stealth field generators]] and [[Skirmish on Yavin Station|attacked]] Tan. Luckily, Revan and his companions [[Death/Legends|killed]] the Trandoshans, and in thanks, Suvam showed Revan his most private stock of merchandise, including [[Mantle of the Force]] and [[Heart of the Guardian]].

==Biography==
{{Quote|I'm kind of out of touch with the rest of the galaxy. I haven't left this system in almost 50 years, and this station in the last five, so... Hey! It's not a bad way to live! I have all the gadgets I need to tinker with and the smugglers and Trandoshans bring me food and interesting pieces of equipment! |Suvam Tan to Revan|<ref name="KotOR" />}}
A former [[Slavery/Legends|slave]] for the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]] during the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]], Suvam was an accomplished inventor, having been apprenticed to a [[Baragwin/Legends|Baragwin]] weaponsmith.<ref name="KotOR" />

At the end of the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Exar Kun War]], the Republic bombarded the surface of Yavin 4, killing Suvam's Baragwin friends. His masters having been killed, Tan was free. He then searched several of the [[Moon/Legends|moons]] of Yavin for salvageable parts, having memorized several bountiful locations during his slave days. Tan took up residence on [[Yavin Station]], a supply depot abandoned by the Republic following the war's end. There he set up a trading post and workshop. He became known as a skilled mechanic and inventor, even attracting the attention of the [[Exchange]]. His inventions were sought after by many, especially [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]]. He even constructed a [[Cortosis/Legends|cortosis]]-resistant [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]].<ref name="Big Red One 5" /> During this time, Suvam scavenged a number of valuable objects on Yavin 4, most notably the legendary crystal artifacts [[Heart of the Guardian]] and [[Mantle of the Force]], which may have been later used by the reformed Revan in his hunt to find the [[Star Forge]].<ref name="KotOR" />

Years laters as the low-quality Yavin Station began to crumble, Suvam hid several of his most prized possessions on locations on the three habitable moons of Yavin. He intended to send a data crystal containing information of where to find these to his only son on [[Rodia/Legends|Rodia]]. However, just as the freighter carrying the data crystal undocked, Yavin Station exploded. The blast propelled it into the [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]] moon [[Yavin 22]], where Suvam's remains were found millennia later by a descendant of his named [[Suz Tanwa]].<ref name="Big Red One 5" />
